title: Disjoint Sets, Connectivity, and Offline Grouping · Counterexample Repair Bay
summary: Track changing network connectivity across merges without scanning every member. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a union-find service with measured compression and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent disjoint sets as parent forests with explicit representative invariants.
Apply union by rank or size without corrupting representative metadata.
Trace path compression and explain its interaction with later finds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
